Dear Mr. Olson: 
September 26, 1989 
Re: 
Advice Letter Dated 
September 19, 1989; 
Our File No. A-89-478 
This is to correct an error in a date in the above-referenced 
letter. 
The last sentence of the second paragraph of that letter 
should read: 
"That statement must be filed no later than January 
31, 1990." 
Thank you for bringing this error to my attention. 
Sincerely,

California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
September 19, 1989 
Lance H. Olson 
Olson, Connelly, Hagel & Fong 
300 Capitol Mall, Ste. 350 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Re: 
Your Request For Advice 
Our File No. A-89-478 
Dear M. Olson: 
You have requested written confirmation of telephone advice 
provided to you concerning the campaign provisions of the 
Political Reform Act (Government Code Sections 81000-91015). 
You 
have requested this advice on behalf of your client, Senator John 
Garamendi and Progress in the 90's, his controlled committee. 
Your letter correctly states my advice that the first 
campaign disclosure statement required to be filed by Progress in 
~the 90's is the semi-annual statement covering the period ending 
December 31, 1989. 
That statement must be filed no later than 
January 1, 1990. 
me. 
If you have any questions about this letter, please contact 
Sincerely, 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
General Counsel/!

AUG to 3 GO PH 'a9 
LANCE H. OLSON 
BRUCE ]. HAGEL 
LEROY Y. FONG 
ROBERT E. LEIDIGH 
OF COUNSEL 
LLOYD C. CONNELLY, Member 
California State Legislature 
300 Capitol Mall, Suite 350 
Sacramento, California 95814 
TELEPHONE: (916) 442-2952 
FAX: (916) 442-1280 
August 9, 1989 
Jeanne Pritchard 
Chief( Technical 
Assistance and Analysis Division 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
428 J Street, Suite 800 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
RE: 
Senator John Garamendi -
Progress in the 90's, A 
Garamendi committee 
Dear Ms. Pritchard: 
The purpose of this letter is to confirm our 
telephone conversation of July 20, 1989 concerning the 
filing obligations under the Political Reform Act for 
the above-referenced committee. 
In our telephone conversation I advised you that 
the Progress In the 90's, A Garamendi Committee was a 
recipient committee registered with the Secretary of 
State earlier this year for the purpose of qualifying 
and supporting the passage of a state-wide ballot 
measure to amend the Gann limit in the state 
constitution. 
In prior oral advice given to me as 
treasurer for that committee, you indicated that 
because the committee was a ballot measure 
qualificiation committee, the committee would not be 
required to file a semi-annual campaign report on or 
before July 31, 1989 for the period ending June 30, 
1989. 
In our most recent telephone conversation I 
advised you that on July 10, 1989, Governor Dukemejian 
signed bills that included legislation to place on the 
state ballot next June a measure which would amend the 
Gann limit. 
This legislative ballot measure would 
accomplish precisely the goal for which the Progress In 
the 90's committee originally was established. 
Consequently, the Progress In the 90's committee's 
purpose will no longer be the qualification of a state-
wide ballot measure. 
Instead, the committee will 
expend funds in support of the passage of the measure. 


" . 
Jeanne Pritchard 
August 9, 1989 
Page 2 
You advised that because the nature of the 
committee had not changed prior to June 30, 1989, the 
committee would not be required to file a semi-annual 
campaign statement on or before July 31, 1989. 
Instead, the committee would file its first report on 
or before January 31, 1989 for the period ending 
December 31, 1989. 
I would appreciate your confirmation of this 
advise at your earliest convenience. 
Very truly yours, 
OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL & FONG 
LANCE H. OLSON 
LHO/jph

California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
August 21, 1989 
Lance H. Olson 
Olson, Connelly, Hagel & Fong 
300 Capitol Mall, Suite 350 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Re: 
Letter No. 89-478 
Dear Mr. Olson: 
We received your letter requesting confirmation of advice 
under the Political Reform Act on August 10, 1989. 
Your letter 
has been assigned to our Technical Assistance and Analysis 
Division for response. 
If you have any questions, you may contact 
that division directly at (916) 322-5662. 
If the letter is appropriate for confirmation without further 
analysis, we will attempt to expedite our response. 
A confirming 
response will be released after it has gone through our approval 
process. 
If the letter is not appropriate for this treatment, the 
staff person assigned to prepare the response will contact you 
shortly to advise you. 
In such cases, the normal analysis, review 
and approval process will be followed. 
You should be aware that your letter and our response are 
public records which may be disclosed to any interested person 
upon receip~ of a proper request for disclosure. 
KED:plh:confadv1 
Sincerely, 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
General Counsel 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804,0807 • (916)322,5660

sections.question: Because the nature of the committee had not changed prior to June 30, 1989, would the committee be required to file a semi-annual campaign statement on or before July 31, 1989, or would it instead file its first report on or before January 31, 1990 for the period ending December 31, 1989?
sections.conclusion: That statement must be filed no later than January 31, 1990.

sections.question_synthetic: What are the campaign disclosure filing requirements for Progress in the 90's committee, a ballot measure committee that changed its purpose from ballot qualification to support of a legislative ballot measure?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The first campaign disclosure statement required to be filed by Progress in the 90's is the semi-annual statement covering the period ending December 31, 1989, which must be filed no later than January 31, 1990.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Document contains multiple related correspondence pieces: incoming request from attorney (August 9, 1989), FPPC receipt acknowledgment (August 21, 1989), initial FPPC response (September 19, 1989), and correction letter (September 26, 1989). Original response had a date error (January 1, 1990 corrected to January 31, 1990) that was corrected in follow-up letter. OCR quality is good overall.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Because the nature of the committee had not changed prior to June 30, 1989, would the committee be required to file a semi-annual campaign statement on or before July 31, 1989, or would it instead file its first report on or before January 31, 1990 for the period ending December 31, 1989?

CONCLUSION: That statement must be filed no later than January 31, 1990.

embedding.summary: FPPC provided advice to Senator John Garamendi's controlled committee regarding campaign disclosure filing obligations under the Political Reform Act, confirming that the committee's first required report would be the semi-annual statement for the period ending December 31, 1989. A subsequent correction letter clarified the filing deadline as January 31, 1990.
